heroHeadline: Day 64: Senate CIA Hearing With Maru Campos Tomorrow; Fractura Total Day 17 — Dual CJNG Factions in Sustained Buildup; 45 Days to FIFA World Cup 2026
heroSubtitle: Day 64 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa, Jalisco (February 22, 2026). Fractura Total Day 17 (declared April 11): The El Jardinero/El Sapo vs. O3/Los Cuinis internal war remains in sustained dual-buildup — no direct inter-faction combat confirmed in the CJNG Jalisco heartland through Day 17. 45 days to FIFA World Cup 2026 opening match (June 11, Mexico City). CIA SOVEREIGNTY CRISIS — SENATE HEARING TOMORROW (April 28): Mexico's Senate hearing with Chihuahua Governor Maru Campos and Mexico City AG Ernestina Godoy before the Constitutional Points and Public Security commissions is confirmed for 11:00 hours on April 28, 2026 — the institutional apex of the governance crisis triggered by the April 20 CIA vehicle crash. The hearing will determine whether the Senate pursues formal sanctions on Chihuahua or codifies Sheinbaum's April 24-25 governance reform into binding federal policy. POLITICAL BATTLE (April 26): PAN president Jorge Romero issued a formal statement reaffirming 'absolute and total support' for Governor Maru Campos; PRI also defended the governor. Both parties contested Sheinbaum's characterization of Chihuahua as 'the main failure.' The CIA sovereignty crisis has crystallized into a ruling Morena vs. opposition PAN/PRI constitutional dispute over state-level bilateral security cooperation authority. APRIL VIOLENCE SURGE: 273 cartel attacks documented nationally in April 2026 (ACLED/Homeland Security Today) — a sharp escalation tracking post-Mencho fragmentation and the fractura total buildup. CJNG remained the nationally dominant source of organized criminal activity. FRACTURA TOTAL DAY 17 STATUS: El Jardinero's 'Monstruos' armored vehicle deployments across the Jalisco-Zacatecas border near Colotlán; O3 retains Grupo Élite command and Los Cuinis financial infrastructure. Both factions in pre-combat positioning consistent with historical cartel mobilization patterns before major offensive escalation. OPERATIVO ENJAMBRE: 80+ arrests cumulative; 1,146 years sentenced in Edomex (April 25). El 85 sentencing: July 31, 2026.
footerNote: Data sourced from SEDENA, SSPC, FGR, DEA, U.S. DOJ, NCTC, U.S. Treasury (OFAC), InSight Crime, Crisis Group, Wilson Center, Reuters, AP, NPR, Wall Street Journal, El País, Washington Post, CBS News, Al Jazeera, UPI, South China Morning Post, CGTN, France 24, Latin Times, Infobae México, La Silla Rota, WOLA, Milenio, Excélsior, Proceso, El Financiero, El Imparcial, Borderland Beat, RED Michoacán, Semanario ZETA, La Voz de la Frontera, TV Azteca Baja California, Time, CNN, ACLED, OCCRP, El Heraldo de México, La Razón de México, Mexico News Daily, The Yucatan Times, Expansion Política, PAN, Homeland Security Today, and major Mexican news outlets. All contested figures noted. Last update: April 27, 2026.
